record.pub: PBS 10/2 3, pp.118-20; Berkman, ZA 57, pp.430f.(T); Falkenstein, MDOG 85, p.9ff.(not yet checked)
BE 30/1 9, p. 42, pls. 5-6; Harper's mark; Ni. 22-2-10-89

record.func: Therapeutic: releasing internal illness (šà-gig) caused by spell (na)
Therapeutic or protective: scorpion's tail as remedy for impotence or as physical threat

record.pub: Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 340-341, tav. XXXVII [1979]; Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla l, 21-23 [1984]
record.func: Therapeutic or protective: scorpion's tail as remedy for impotence or as physical threat

record.pub: Matthiae, P., Ebla: Un impero inciso nell’argilla (2nd ed.), tav. 64 (obv.) [1979]; Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 342-343, tav. XXXVIII [1979]; Matthiae, P., Ebla: Un impero inciso nell’argilla,, tav. 47 (obv.) [1979]; Mander, P., Or. 48, 335 n. 2, 336-339 [1979]; Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla 1.24 [1984]; Matthiae, P., I tesori di Ebla, tav. 27c (obv.) [1984]
record.func: Therapeutic or protective: scorpion's tail as remedy for impotence or as physical threat
Possibly therapeutic: releasing spell (na) in Text 9a, incantation (mu -- syllabic writing for mu7) in Text 9b
Possibly therapeutic: releasing spell (na) in Text 9a, incantation (mu -- syllabic writing for mu7) in Text 9b
Protective: protecting against snakes and scorpions
Protective: protecting against snakes and scorpions

record.gods: NE.DAG, father (of the deities) and his offspring
record.func: Therapeutic: releasing internal illness (šà-gig) possibly caused by incantation (mu7/tu6)
Therapeutic: releasing eye-ache (igi-gig) caused by Enki
Possibly therapeutic: similar to therapeutic Texts 30 and 31
record.feat: Features praise of tamarisk as divine purifier

record.func: Temple-foundation: opening to royal inscription recording foundation of temple; more specifically, possibly used for opening the mouths of divine statues
record.feat: Features praise of reed as divine purifier

record.func: Therapeutic: releasing internal illness (ša-gi -- syllabic writing for šà-gig), illness (dur -- syllabic writing for tur5) and bile (zé), caused by spell (na) and incantation (du -- syllabic writing for tu6)

record.pub: Pettinato, G., OrAnt. 18, 339, tav. XXXVI [1979]; Krebernik, M., Die Beschwörungen aus Fara und Ebla 19 [1984]
record.gods: An, Enki and Ninki, Enlil, Utu
record.func: Therapeutic: releasing illness (gig) possibly caused by incantation (du -- syllabic writing for tu6)
record.feat: Features praise of tamarisk as divine purifier, divine oath and oath by heaven and the underworld

record.func: Therapeutic: references to neck problem (gú-kéš), the evil ala and possibly the udug

record.subs: Subscript: én-é-nu-ru munus-a-kam (It is an incantation for a woman)

record.func: Therapeutic: releasing illness caused by snake, scorpion and dog
record.subs: Subscript: lú muš zú ba-dù a-bi lú-kúr-ra nag-nag-da-kam (It is for making a bitten man -- a man a snake has bitten -- drink this water)
